free_text: Optimization of isotope production by cross section
determination
Thick target yields and contamination ratios
for enriched targets were derived.
Target stacks of up to 15 foils with an exposed
surface of 20 mm diameter were irradiated for 6 min
at 3 muA in external colimated proton beams.
Coaxial Ge(Li) detector (10% relative
efficiency, 2 keV FWHM resolution at 1332 keV).
Efficiency calibration for different measuring
geometries was performed using standard 57Co, 133Ba,
152Eu and 22Na sources.
Corrections for decay during bombardment, waiting
time and measuring time were made.
The overall uncertainty in cross sections as
derived from the quadratic summation of the
uncertainties is 8.5%.
The error in the target thickness is 6 %.
The error in integrated beam current is 2%.
The error in the counting statistics is 2%.
The error in the detector efficiency is 5%.
The error in the physical decay data is 5%.
EN-ERR - maximal error on energy values of data
points is 0.6 to 1.0 MeV due to uncertainties on
incident energy and energy degradation.
Tbl.1
